summary: Closed, unmerged MP subsystem split from a larger matching-improvement branch. The slice touched src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c and src/melee/mp/mplib.c, attempting a stack/local declaration adjustment in mpColl_80046224_LeftWall and a data-layout change that merged several spawn vertex ID arrays into one larger mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds array with pointer-like macros for enemy, trophy, and exit IDs. Reviewer feedback said the changes looked incorrect: the defines implied wrong field/array sizing based on offsets, and using defines this way was considered a regression, so the PR was closed.
what_changed: In src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c, moved the declarations of Vec3 nrm and PAD_STACK(0x44) upward inside mpColl_80046224_LeftWall, before mpLeftWallGetTop and before the conditional that previously contained them.

In src/melee/mp/mplib.c, replaced separate static s16 arrays mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds[0x15], mpLib_EnemySpawnVtxIds[0x50], mpLib_TrophySpawnVtxIds[0x19], and mpLib_ExitVtxIds[0x2E] with one larger static s16 mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds[0xAE].
Inserted explicit zero separators in the merged mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds data between the former item/enemy/trophy/exit regions.
Added macros mapping the former array names to offsets inside mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds: mpLib_EnemySpawnVtxIds at +0x16, mpLib_TrophySpawnVtxIds at +0x66, and mpLib_ExitVtxIds at +0x80.
The PR was created as an isolated path slice for src/melee/mp from a larger branch, with the body reporting no broken matches in the unsplit post-rebase report, but it was closed after review.
smart_moves: The PR was scoped narrowly to src/melee/mp and split from a larger branch to make subsystem review easier.
The body documented local hygiene checks, including applying the slice from origin/master and passing git diff --check.

The diff attempted to preserve existing symbol names for former spawn vertex arrays through aliases, which suggests an effort to keep call sites/source references stable while changing data layout; however, reviewer feedback judged this approach incorrect.
decomp_lessons: Do not merge adjacent data arrays into one backing array purely to force a match unless the symbol sizes, offsets, and referenced field sizes are independently supported by evidence.
Macro aliases for data symbols can be a regression in decomp code when they hide real object boundaries or imply incorrect array sizes.
When reconstructing static data, array lengths and offset relationships matter as much as byte contents; reviewers may reject a byte-layout trick if it misrepresents source-level objects.

Stack/PAD_STACK relocation in matching work should be treated cautiously and reviewed against generated code, since moving locals across control-flow scopes can alter stack lifetime assumptions even if the local is only used later.
Directory-split PRs still need each slice to be semantically defensible on its own; green CI or an unsplit branch report is not enough if the slice introduces questionable source modeling.
naming_conventions: Existing MP collision function names use the mpColl_800NNNNN_DescriptiveName form, as shown by mpColl_80046224_LeftWall.

MP library helper/data names use the mpLib_ prefix, including mpLib_DrawCrosses, mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds, mpLib_EnemySpawnVtxIds, mpLib_TrophySpawnVtxIds, mpLib_ExitVtxIds, mpLib_SpawnVtxIds, and mpLib_RespawnVtxIds.
Collision line constants in the excerpt use CollLine_ names such as CollLine_Ceiling and CollLine_LeftWall.

The proposed macro aliases reused former data-symbol names exactly, but review feedback indicates that preserving names via preprocessor defines is not necessarily acceptable when it obscures real data layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The mpcoll.c hunk used local declaration and PAD_STACK placement as a stack-shaping tactic around Vec3 nrm in mpColl_80046224_LeftWall.
The mplib.c hunk used contiguous static data coalescing plus offset macros as a data-layout/matching tactic.
The reviewer explicitly rejected the define-based tactic because the offsets/sizes appeared wrong and the define usage was considered a regression.
Future matching work should prefer accurately declared static objects over macro-offset aliases unless there is strong binary evidence that the original source modeled the data that way.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.text: Overall, these changes look incorrect. The defines even show that the size changes of the particular fields being referenced would be wrong based on offset.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.text: Likewise, the usage of the define is a regression, so I'm closing this PR.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching mpLib_ItemSpawnVtxIds, mpLib_EnemySpawnVtxIds, mpLib_TrophySpawnVtxIds, or mpLib_ExitVtxIds for the accepted data-layout model.
Search for subsequent accepted changes to mpColl_80046224_LeftWall to see whether the Vec3 nrm/PAD_STACK placement was fixed differently.
Compare the generated map/symbol sizes for the spawn vertex ID arrays before attempting another coalescing or split-data change.
Look for project guidance or review precedent on using #define aliases for data symbols in matching PRs.
